helpful: Provisioned filed-document text was decisive: the BIO's concession that the petition should be held for Salazar v. Paramount Global settled the disposition shape without any external retrieval.
Statpack per-Term and per-circuit cuts gave usable anchors for the residual probability mass.
gaps: fedcourts query --topic returned zero rows for every topical probe (VPPA, privacy, statutory interpretation), so no similar-prior retrieval was possible; topic matching seems too sparse for issue-based lookup.

No corpus signal for held-pending-lead-case petitions: a cut of dispositions for petitions held for a granted companion QP (GVR vs deny split) would be the exactly-relevant base rate for cells like this one.
The predict prompt references a statpack 'Segment base rate by salience band' table that is not present in the committed metrics/statpack.md.
CourtListener MCP does not cover supremecourt.gov dockets, so the lead case's SCOTUS-side status (e.g. argument date for 25-459) could not be checked.
notes: Forward cell whose outcome is almost entirely a function of a different, already-granted case (Salazar, No. 25-459). The binary probability here is really P(reversal in the lead case); scoring will lag until Salazar is decided (~June 2027).
